skill.name: expo-api-audit
skill.description: Audits Expo and React Native API integration layers for authentication, generated API client usage, hardcoded data, offline behavior, and backend synchronization gaps.
skill.summary: Audit Expo API usage, auth flows, generated hooks, and offline behavior.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and environment-variable patterns, but review shows they are Markdown audit examples and search commands in SKILL.md, not executable skill code. The dangerous exfiltration heuristic is a false positive because no credential collection, network transmission, obfuscation, or prompt injection attempt was found. Publish with a warning because the skill directs agents to run shell commands such as find, rg, grep, xargs, npx, and npm in a user project.

medium_findings.title: Guided Shell Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run project-wide shell commands for audit discovery, including find, rg, grep, xargs, npx orval --dry-run, and optional global package installation. These commands are legitimate for an API audit but can execute tools in the target project environment, so users should review commands before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly contains shell command blocks and dependency-install guidance. The context is audit discovery rather than malicious execution, so the risk is operational and permission-related.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network and Credential Exfiltration Heuristic
low_findings.description: Static analysis combined fetch, axios, URL, process.env, and command tokens into a critical exfiltration heuristic. Manual review found these are search patterns, example findings, and Expo configuration guidance inside Markdown, with no code that sends credentials or project data to an external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside explanatory Markdown or command examples. No executable implementation file, outbound request with secret data, or obfuscation mechanism was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding appears to come from text in the skill metadata or Markdown wording, not from a hashing or encryption implementation. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, or other weak crypto being used to protect data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain frontmatter and overview text for an API audit skill. There is no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive implementation in the file.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Project Reconnaissance Commands
low_findings.description: The skill asks agents to inspect project files, API clients, screens, stores, and config files. This is expected for an audit skill, but it exposes local project structure and configuration to the active AI session.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reconnaissance commands are explicit and mostly read-only. They are legitimate for the stated purpose, but users should run the skill only in repositories they intend to audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Examples Require User Consent
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown command blocks include shell loops, xargs pipelines, npx execution, and package installation suggestions. These are not hidden payloads, but marketplace users should understand that the skill relies on running local tooling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risky patterns are visible command examples with a clear audit purpose. The remaining risk depends on the safety of the target project and installed tools.

content.value_statement: Expo apps can drift from their API contracts and hide auth or sync problems. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a focused audit of API clients, tokens, screens, stores, and offline behavior.
actual_capabilities: Maps Expo API clients, generated hooks, token managers, Zustand stores, and screen data sources.
Checks whether authentication tokens use expo-secure-store instead of unsafe storage patterns.
Finds raw fetch or axios calls that bypass generated OpenAPI or Orval hooks.
Reviews forms, buttons, and mutations to confirm user changes sync to the backend.
Assesses offline behavior, cache persistence, reconnect handling, and query error states.
Produces severity-based audit reports with specific remediation guidance.
limitations: It is an audit guide, not an automated scanner with guaranteed complete coverage.
Results depend on project structure, available local tools, and the agent following the checklist carefully.
Some commands may need adjustment for nonstandard Expo, monorepo, or generated-client layouts.
It does not prove backend behavior unless the API contract or server code is available.

use_cases.title: Pre-release mobile API review
use_cases.description: Review an Expo app before release to find auth, generated-client, and backend synchronization gaps.

use_cases.title: Hardcoded data cleanup
use_cases.description: Find screens, stores, and handlers that still use mock data or local-only state.

use_cases.title: Security and offline readiness check
use_cases.description: Assess token storage, API configuration, offline behavior, and error handling in one structured pass.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run an Expo API integration audit for this project. Focus on auth token handling, direct API calls, hardcoded data, and offline behavior. Return a concise Markdown report.

prompt_templates.title: Check auth and config
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the Expo authentication and API configuration layer. Check token storage, refresh handling, API base URLs, expo-constants usage, and production build risks.

prompt_templates.title: Find unsynced screens
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the app screens and stores for data that does not sync to the backend. Flag forms, toggles, buttons, and Zustand state that should use API mutations.

prompt_templates.title: Produce remediation tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full Expo API audit and convert the findings into prioritized engineering tasks. Include severity, affected area, likely fix, and suggested test coverage.

output_examples.input: Audit my Expo app for API integration gaps.
output: Executive summary with counts by severity.
Findings for auth storage, direct API calls, and screens without generated hooks.
Recommended fixes and validation steps for each issue.

output_examples.input: Check whether my profile form really saves to the backend.
output: A trace from screen handler to mutation or API call.
A clear verdict on whether the change persists remotely.
A fix plan if the form only updates local state.

output_examples.input: Review offline behavior in my React Native API layer.
output: Assessment of query cache settings and reconnect behavior.
Notes on mutation behavior while offline.
Recommended user-facing error states and retry paths.

best_practices: Run the audit from the repository root so discovery commands can find screens, stores, config, and generated clients.
Share known problem flows first, especially auth refresh, profile updates, checkout, or offline edits.
Review shell commands before execution and avoid installing global tools without project approval.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every raw fetch or axios call as a bug without checking third-party or upload use cases.
Do not store server entities in Zustand when React Query or generated hooks should own the data.
Do not use process.env directly in Expo production code when app config values are required.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my Expo app?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to inspect files and produce audit findings unless you ask for fixes.

faq.question: Which projects fit this skill best?
faq.answer: It fits Expo and React Native TypeScript apps using API clients, generated hooks, TanStack Query, Zustand, or Expo Router.

faq.question: Can it find insecure token storage?
faq.answer: Yes. It specifically checks for token use in AsyncStorage and recommends expo-secure-store patterns.

faq.question: Can it audit Orval or OpenAPI generation?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks generated hook coverage, mutator configuration, and endpoint alignment with the API contract.

faq.question: Does it require ripgrep?
faq.answer: No. Ripgrep is recommended for speed, and the skill includes grep-style alternatives.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use it only where the AI session is allowed to inspect project code and configuration. The skill does not include exfiltration code.
